company.name: TED
company.notes: Educational content. Large brand. Some distributed roles.

research.status_summary: TED is actively operating and selectively hiring (~3–6 open roles as of May 2026), with no public layoff announcements, though employee reviews flag ongoing upper-management restructuring and historically limited remote access outside the tech team.
research.hiring_policy: TED posts US-based roles (New York HQ) and explicitly notes at least one grant-sponsored position open to remote US applicants; visa sponsorship policy is not publicly stated, and most operational roles appear to require on-site presence, making EOR-friendly placement unlikely without direct confirmation.
recent_news: TED2026 'All of Us' ran April 13–17 in Vancouver — its final flagship conference there before relocating to San Diego, CA in 2027; CEO/head curator Chris Anderson stepped down following the event.
TED Countdown co-hosted a 2026 CarbonX event with Tencent spotlighting climate-tech winners in carbon capture, clean energy storage, and industrial-scale climate solutions.
TEDAI Vienna is scheduled for October 28–30, 2026, TED's dedicated annual AI conference, curated in collaboration with TED's main organization.
research.growth_signal: TED is in a strategic transition phase — relocating its flagship conference, turning over senior leadership, and expanding its AI and climate programming verticals — but headcount remains small and growth is content/brand-driven rather than funded headcount scaling.
research.fit_for_lior: TED's heavy 2026 focus on Creative AI (Kelly Boesch AI music talk, AI-creativity intersection programming) and its podcast/audience-growth analyst roles (grant-funded, data-to-action mandates) map well to Lior's Creative AI and Product Analyst threads, especially given TED's brand reach and distributed content roles.
